task_yaml.task:
I read Hacker News (https://news.ycombinator.com/) and I want to pull data
from it programmatically. The website itself doesn't expose an obvious API on
the page, but there is an official public data API for Hacker News.

Please find that official API and use it (not by scraping the
news.ycombinator.com HTML) to do two things:

1. Fetch the current front-page top stories list, take the #1 story right
   now, and report its id, title, url (if any), author (`by`), score, and
   number of comments (`descendants`).
2. As a fixed cross-check, also fetch the item with id **8863** and report
   its title and author — this is a specific historical item so its core
   fields won't change.

Save the result to `/tmp_workspace/results/hackernews.json` (a `top_story`
object and an `item_8863` object), and save the exact request URL(s) you
called plus their raw JSON response(s) to
`/tmp_workspace/results/hackernews_evidence/`.

prompt:

2. As a fixed cross-check, also fetch the item with id **8863** and report
